summary.intent: Fix three Codex-identified edge cases in the combobox component around custom-value commits: blur-before-item-click adding filter text, Enter preserving canonical keys instead of custom text, and clearing stale selection on empty custom input.
summary.outcome: All three regressions were fixed via TDD (failing tests added first, then minimal machine changes), 54 combobox unit tests pass green, and changes were committed and pushed as a follow-up to the inline-completion fix (c54e7d7e).

repo: The combobox state machine uses a closure-captured context, so checking ctx.open inside a blur closure reads already-closed state; the pre-blur open state must be captured before building the close transition.
cargo test only accepts a single test filter argument — multiple space-separated test names are silently ignored; use a module-level filter instead.
The repo has existing stable-rustfmt warnings that appear during cargo fmt --check but do not block CI.
Codex review is triggered via '@codex review' PR comment and signals in-progress status with a 👀 reaction; completion is indicated by the reaction being removed and/or new review threads appearing.

The project uses llvm-cov for coverage (cargo llvm-cov test) and has a multi-job CI matrix including clippy, formatting, mutual-exclusion, snapshot, unit, adapter, coverage, release verification, and feature-flag variants (Dioxus, Leptos, subsystems).

code.finding: CommitInput path applied custom text even when input matched the label of an already-selected item, causing canonical key to be overwritten with display label.
Blur handler closure checked ctx.open after close transition was already applied, making the open-state check always see closed; fix requires capturing open state before building the transition.
Multi-select blur committed filter text as a custom value before the subsequent item-click event could fire, resulting in spurious additions to the selection.

workflow: TDD cycle: add focused regression tests that reproduce the exact Codex findings (RED), then apply minimal machine changes (GREEN) before committing — avoids fixing the wrong thing.
Pre-commit verification sequence: cargo fmt --check, git diff --check, cargo clippy -p <crate> --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings, cargo llvm-cov test scoped to affected module.
Codex review polling used 2-minute sleep intervals with combined reaction + thread + CI checks in a single shell compound to minimize round trips.
Inline review thread replies include the fixing commit SHA for traceability before resolving threads.

friction: cargo test silently ignores multiple space-separated test name filters — only the first is used, requiring a switch to module-level filtering.
Blur closure captured stale context state, requiring a non-obvious fix (pre-capture open flag) that was only discovered after the first GREEN attempt still had one failing test.
Codex review polling required ~8 two-minute sleep cycles (~16 minutes) before findings appeared, with no way to get an earlier signal than the 👀 reaction drop.
open_items: CI coverage, release verification, and Dioxus feature-flag jobs were still pending at commit time — final CI green status not confirmed in transcript.
The existing stable-rustfmt warnings that appear during cargo fmt --check were noted but not addressed.
